entry.title: Re: API to Read Change Entity Type in Dynamic BADI
entry.content:
Hi @mines ,

You can use BADI USMD_SSW_RULE_CONTEXT_PREPARE for enhancing the single value decision table and route the workflow according to changes in general data.

Can use API CL_USMD_MODEL_EXT to get the entities changed within CR using method READ_ENTITY_DATA_ALL. 


Regards,

Aditya

entry.title: Essential SAP Enterprise Support enablement to help you get the work done
entry.content:
SAP Enterprise Support Academy is your starting point for practical guidance and outcome-driven learning from SAP.

Whether you are starting a project or in the middle of an SAP task, you want clear guidance and practical steps without digging through multiple platforms. You need support that helps you make progress, make informed decisions, and take the next step with clarity to reach your goals.

You can now find SAP Enterprise Support Academy on learning.sap.com. We bring together learning offerings, guidance, resources and expert live sessions designed to help you plan, implement, and optimize your SAP solutions, so you can move from planning to action and raise the value of your investments more quickly.

The foundation for your success

SAP Enterprise Support is part of SAP’s foundational support offering, providing essential resources and expert support you rely on to keep systems running smoothly and move initiatives forward.

At the center of this offering sits SAP Enterprise Support Academy. It is where foundational support becomes practical enablement, helping you build skills, apply best practices, and raise the value of your SAP solutions by turning learning into real outcomes in your SAP environment.

What you can access

Through SAP Enterprise Support Academy, SAP Enterprise Support and cloud customers have access to learning and guidance aligned to real business scenarios, at no additional cost.

  • Self-paced courses, learning journeys, and videos
  • Live expert-led sessions, including Expert-guided Implementation (EGI)
  • Guidance that supports planning, implementation, and ongoing improvement
  • Access using your SAP Universal ID (S-user ID required)

This content was previously available as the SAP Enterprise Support edition of SAP Learning Hub.

Learn in the way that fits your work

Self-paced learning
Use courses, learning journeys, and videos to build knowledge when it fits your schedule, whether you need a quick answer or a deeper understanding.

Live sessions
Join webcasts or hands-on Expert-guided Implementation sessions to work through topics with SAP experts and ask questions relevant to your situation.

Expert-guided Implementation: learning that supports execution
Expert-guided Implementation (EGI) sessions are hands-on, remote workshops designed to help you apply learning directly in your SAP environment.

Each session follows a simple, effective approach:

  • An SAP expert explains a defined activity step by step
  • You carry out the activity directly in your own SAP system
  • Guidance is available while you work

This format helps shorten timelines, reduce rework, and help you execute with fewer surprises.

Need direction, not just content?

Value maps on SAP Community bring together guidance, recommended actions, and supporting resources to help you decide what to focus on next.

Visit us today

If you are looking for practical learning, clear guidance and expert support, it is now all in one place.

👉 Visit the SAP Enterprise Support Academy page on learning.sap.com and quickly search for what you need.

entry.title: Re: SAP Cloud Connector - websocket connection in SAP clo...
entry.content:
Hi Emilien,
we have already achieved the connectivity using the manage message broker and we are already connecting to Hive MQTT using that.
BUT the problem that we are facing is that regarding the turn around timings where Message left from Shop Floor --> coming to MQTT Broker (published to one topic)--> SAP production connector works as subscriber(received it) --> send to SAP DM ( DM process data and generate the response)--> send to Production connector (works as Client) --> response back to MQTT is very high.

but Business requirement is to have turn around time is very less(<200ms). in this scenario, Publish MQTT Message itself is taking more then 400~500 ms. so is there any way we can reduce the turn around time <200 ms.

Regards,

Ankit Gupta

entry.title: Re: SAP Cloud Connector - websocket connection in SAP clo...
entry.content:
Hi @Ulrich_Schmidt1 ,

As of now, our MQTT broker is running on WebSocket 8884 port and when we are trying to make connection the SAP Cloud connector over the HTTP/S protocol, it always says not reachable, even though we are able to connect via MQTT Explorer with same configuration.

 

Please let us know if there is any way we can connect to explain the issue more in details. or needed, we can raise the OSS to SAP for the same.

Regards,
Ankit Gupta

entry.title: Re: Pay Component Rate for Time Account Payout
entry.content:
Hi Cindy,

I was looking for the same information and to my surprise couldn't find anything about it.

However: From System behaviour, it seems to calculate as follows:

The annualized salary (Details on how that is calculated can be found in 2162127 - Compensation Information: Pay Component Group Calculations - SAP for Me) from the PayComponentGroup that you maintain in the Time Account Payout Profile is first divided by 52 (weeks) and then divided by the "Average Working Days per Week" that you maintain in the Workschedule (see Leave Payout (Admin Scenario Only) | SAP Help Portal) - if the Time Account Type that you want to payout  has "Day(s)" as Unit. (If it's hours, I suppose the second divisor is the "Average Hours Per Week" from the Workschedule.)

I hope that helps.

Kind regards

Peter

entry.title: Re: Onboarding - Your HR manager has requested a correcti...
Re: Not Getting Email Notifications for Comments Posted o...
entry.content:
Hi @Abhijit_Shinde, to move the conversation from the comments section to an answer 🙂
We currently don't have other users reporting similar problems with email notifications. I will send you an email so that we can troubleshoot further to see if we can find the root cause of this. I will add a comment to the blog post you linked to in your question, to see if that triggers a notification or not.

entry.title: Re: Managing Invoices with SAP Ariba Central Invoice Management

entry.title: Re: SAP Logistics Management
entry.content:
Hello,

SAP Logistics Management can be integrated with SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition. You can find more information in this SAP Note: 3602278 - Integration of SAP Logistics Management with SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition via SAP Integration Suite.

The solution requires additional subscription.

 

Regards,.

Jose M.

entry.title: Re: Batch restricted status-posting date change
entry.content: Through the debugging of MIGO, movement type 341 is not allowed for MIGO and MB1B transactions.

entry.title: Create an instance of SAP Data Privacy Integration (NextGen) and Digital Manufacturing
entry.content:
Dear all,

as subsriber of SAP Digital Manufacturing we seem eligible for SAP Data Privacy Integration (NextGen?) with service plan data-privacy-internal.

When I try to create an instance of said service plan in the dm subaccount, I am presented an instance creation wizard with an JSON parameter tab, sure enough completely empty / not prefilled whatsoever. If I try to create the instance without giving parameters, sure enough, creation fails 🙄

The helpfile for the paremters do not help me much. So may question goes:

What parameters do I need to key in to create a service instance of data privacy integration in the context of digital manufacturing

Any help greatly appreciated. Much obliged 🤠

Cheers

J

entry.title: Re: error when trying to consume an an orchestration with gen_ai_hub

entry.title: LGM Knowledge Snippet: Action Required Feature Overview
entry.content:
Complex logistics processes involve many steps, systems, and actors. When something goes wrong or requires manual intervention, how do you ensure users find and address these situations quickly? Documents related to transportation planning have multiple statuses that indicate the document's state in the document flow. By their values, you can determine if there's an error. But let's face it — juggling multiple filters for different statuses and remembering to check each one for errors isn't exactly efficient. The Action Required feature in SAP Logistics Management streamlines this process with a clear, actionable signal that helps users focus on what matters.

What is "Action Required"?

The Action Required indicator is a status flag on Transportation Requests and Consignments that signals when user intervention is needed. It always reflects the current state of the logistics process, regardless of whether changes come from users, integrations, or automated workflows.

Think of it as a smart notification system built directly into your logistics documents, ensuring that exceptions and manual tasks never slip through the cracks.

When a Transportation Request or Consignment needs attention, the system sets this indicator with one of the corresponding values: Error or Open Task. This allows users to quickly filter and prioritize their work, focusing on documents that need immediate attention rather than manually checking every document or filtering by a specific status.

Finding Documents That Need Attention

Based on the Action Required indicator, rows in the list report are highlighted with red for Error and orange for Open Task in both the Transportation Request and Consignment management applications. Users can:

  • Filter lists to show only documents requiring action
  • See at a glance which documents need intervention
  • Open the corresponding status popover to quickly assess the type of action needed

When Does Action Become Required?

The system sets the Action Required indicator in several key scenarios:

1. Carrier Determination Failures

When the system cannot automatically determine a suitable carrier for a Transportation Request or Consignment, the Action Required indicator is set with reason code Error. This typically happens when:

  • Freight tendering cannot successfully determine a carrier — whether the tendering process is canceled or no carrier responds
  • Carrier determination rules don't find a matching carrier for the shipment requirements

In these cases, a logistics planner needs to manually assign a carrier. If desired, business rules can be reviewed and adjusted to allow automatic determination for similar scenarios going forward.

 

2. Subcontracting

  • Manual start. For Direct LTL shipping process, subcontracting should be started manually. When a carrier is assigned to the consignment by any means (you can read more about how we determine carriers in this post), the system sets Action Required with reason code Open Task.

     

  • Manual Confirmation. If the consignment carrier does not have a configured integration with Business Network for Logistics (BN4L), the logistics planner must manually confirm subcontracting to continue the process. The system marks the consignment with Action Required (Open Task) to highlight this manual step. In the image below, all four consignments have pending subcontracting confirmation. However, only the first consignment will automatically receive the response from the carrier via BN4L (indicated by a tag in the carrier column) and is therefore not marked as requiring action. The other three consignments are highlighted in orange, signaling that manual confirmation is needed.

     

  • Rejected, Canceled subcontracting. In case subcontracting was rejected by the carrier or canceled by the shipper for some reason, we cannot proceed with the current consignment and highlight this as Error. In that case, to continue the process, the consignment should be recreated and another carrier assigned.

     

3. Pickup/Delivery Document Assignment Errors

To properly manage the transportation activities regarding the pickup, Transportation Requests need to be assigned to Pickup/Delivery Documents (PDDs) to coordinate loading operations. If this assignment fails (for example, when no suitable PDD exists for the carrier, location, and time combination) the system sets Action Required with reason code Error.

Users can then either create the missing PDD manually or wait for automatic PDD creation based on schedule and retry the assignment.

4. Transportation Request Unassignment

When a Transportation Request is manually unassigned from its Consignment, the system marks it with Action Required (reason: Open Task). This ensures the orphaned Transportation Request doesn't get lost and prompts the logistics planner to reassign it to an appropriate Consignment.

Wrapping Up

The Action Required feature exemplifies a key principle of SAP Logistics Management: guide users clearly through complex processes, automate where possible, and surface exceptions in an actionable way. By intelligently detecting when manual intervention is needed, the system ensures users always have an accurate, up-to-date view of what requires their attention.

Whether you're managing hundreds of shipments per day or handling complex multi-modal logistics, the Action Required indicator helps ensure nothing falls through the cracks.

entry.title: Re: Stored procedure using INSERT...LOCATION fails with SQLSTATE QHA02 after RHEL upgrade
entry.content: Dear Alexander, Thank you for your post. Do you use any kind of authentication protocol? If yes, please share details. The issue happens with all the users? What happens if you delete and recreate the external login of IQ? Thank you! Antonio..

entry.title: Re: SAP Analytics Cloud infrastructure is moving to SAP HANA Cloud
entry.content:
Oleksandr,

There isn't yet a way to check when individual tenants will be migrated. We are just beginning the mass migrations and expect them to continue throughout 2026. As we progress we are looking at ways to keep customers informed both pre and post migration of the status of tenants.

entry.title: Re: IAS and Onboarding
entry.content: Hi Gopi - When do customers have to implement IAS with Onboarding? Is it 2H 2026?

entry.title: Re: Datasphere: Hiding dimensions used for filtering from...
entry.content: correct, but then you cannot pass the filter value from the Story down to the model. So this will work only for "hard coded" filters.

entry.title: Error while asset purchase entry
entry.content:
Hi Team, I am newly started learning sap fico and I have configured asset accounting and while activate asset accounting it showing as posting not possible at client due to this I am unable to post asset purchase entry please let us know if you know the solution for this. 

It would be very helpful if you suggest what to be done.

 

Snapshot attached for your reference

entry.title: Re: Datasphere: Hiding dimensions used for filtering from...
entry.content:
More clumsy alternative

MAT_SALES__0VENDOR materialize in the Model-View/FACT-Level. Use Parameter in the View.
Do not use the MAT_SALES__0VENDOR in Model self, but pass the Parameter down as Variable.

This will however cause runtime delay of 2-3 seconds to fill the variables in Story, but you will be able to pass filter values from the Story self

entry.title: Recruiting report identifying # of resumes by gender in each stage of the process - by requisition
entry.content: Has anyone created a report in recruiting that identifies the total number of resumes in each stage of the process by requisition ID and gender?

Thanks

entry.title: Re: #CoE CSRI How to manage data restore in public cloud?
entry.content:
Dear @ericyu,

In Public Cloud the customer can’t do a database restore yourself, SAP runs and manages backups as part of the SaaS service

SAP Note below:

https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/3396152

Only in exceptional / emergency situations, SAP states that restore is not a standard on-demand service for customers. It’s considered only to mitigate serious logical errors for example data corruption or other irreparable situations and each request is reviewed first including whether manual remediation is feasible

So, if it’s a normal posting mistake, wrong interface run, wrong customizing, etc., SAP will typically expect you to correct it functionally reversal/cancellation, corrective postings, reprocessing, etc. rather than restoring the whole tenant

SAP documents the backup strategy roughly as:

  • Production: daily data backups + archive logs multiple times per day, with ~30 days retention
  • Non-production: weekly data backups + archive logs multiple times per day, with ~14 days retention

They also note that recovery can be to a point-in-time within the retention window when a restore is approved

Practical proces:

1. Start to:

    • Identify what is wrong objects/processes impacted and when it started timestamp
    • Stop further damage pause interfaces/jobs/users if needed
    • Decide if manual remediation is possible

2. Open a case with SAP (SAP for Me or Built-In Support)

    • Create the case against the Production system if PRD access is required SAP explicitly calls out the open it on the system where the issue occurs requirement
    • Provide a CB/CC user for SAP Support to reproduce/analyze
    • Component guidance from SAP, common in Public Cloud:
      • XX-S4C-OPR-INC for technical/ops topics (availability/performance/admin-type issues)
      • XX-S4C-OPR-SRV for service requests ops-related requests

3. In the case, be very specific this makes approval more likely if it truly is irreparable

    • Business impact (who/how many users, what’s stopped)
    • Why remediation isn’t feasible
    • The requested recovery point
    • Confirmation you accept that a restore effectively rolls the tenant back because anything after that point is lost and must be re-entered/replayed

4. SAP review + scheduling

    • SAP says restores are not executed immediately and timing can vary from hours to a couple of days depending on recovery point and system lifecycle events
    • Expect a coordinated downtime window and explicit confirmations

More details in SAP Note below:

https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/2563519

Best Regards,

Dawid

entry.title: Re: SAP Cloud Connector - websocket connection in SAP clo...
entry.content:
Hi Ankit,

check for any error messages in the scc_core.trc when you try to connect. There are many possible problems, like port not open on an intermediate firewall, both sides speak incompatible protocols, untrusted certificates, etc. etc.

entry.title: Re: Subscription with recurring deliveries
entry.content: In that case, one would prefer billing done in SD instead of CI, as SD has standard OOB delivery related billing.. Subscription line with BRIM only becomes a wrapper functionality only to have delivery plan and change process for any delivery scheduling change

entry.title: Re: Integrating IBM MQ On-Premise with SAP Cloud Integration Using the IBM MQ Adapter
entry.content: Thanks for the wonderful aricle @Shashank_K. This is very useful for those of us using #IBM MQ and migrating from SAP PO to SAP CI .

entry.title: Re: SMB Adapter Feature: On-Premise Connectivity via SAP Cloud Connector
entry.content: @Benko You can find it in the connection tab.  See below:

entry.title: Re: Adding Custom Fields to a Fiori App Using Custom Fields and Logic
entry.content:
@abap_Sap 

Great post! Just a quick heads-up: SCFD_EUI is actually designed to transform fields from classic extensibility into key user extensions, rather than creating them from scratch. Adding a small note about this might help prevent any confusion for your readers!

entry.title: reverse setelment of period 12/2025 in period2/2026
entry.content:
today 4.2.2026 my user discover that he had make a mistake when setelment process orders in 12/2025 and whant to reverse the setelement today with setelememt period and posting period 12/2025

how over ob52 period 12 is open , no locked period in opk1 i get error "invalid posting period" how to do what user need ?

thank you

entry.title: Creating a CAP Application for Bound and Unbound ODATA V4.
entry.content:
Purpose
To create a simple test application showing how to use Bound and Unbound ODATA V4 calls
In a CAP Application.

Requirements
Access to the SAP Cloud Business Application Studio
Developers Role to create applications.

Bound and Unbound ODATA V2 and ODATA V4 are supported by SAP with CAP and BAS.
Bound and Unbound are mostly used in SAP Fiori application and UI5. However Bound and Unbound can be used
in CAP and BAS as they also support ODATA V4 and ODATA V2.


Calling the Unbound Function (URI Pattern)

Unbound functions are called using a GET request with the function or function import name appended to the service root URL, and parameters provided in parentheses: 

 

GET /odata/v4/catalog/unboundActionWithParam{ "param1": "a", "param2": "b" }

If we were to try and call the URL with a POST, and we pass in Parm1 and parm2 we would receive an Error HTTP/1.1 405 Method Not Allowed

We can call a POST however we cannot pass in and parameter.

 

Example POST /odata/v4/catalog/Books(1)/CatalogService.boundActionWithoutParam

 

Example in a real SAP environment

For an unbound function returning a string involves a service root /odata/v4/catalog and a function product Info with an id parameter, called GET odata/v4/catalog/unboundFunctionWithParam(param1='a',param2='b'). This allows executing custom logic independently of an entity.
Additional details can be found in the SAP Help Portal documentation. Documentation · OData - the Best Way to REST

 What is unbound ODATA V4.

An unbound function is defined in the OData metadata XML with the attribute and a Parameter that specifies the binding type. 

 

GET /odata/v4/catalog/unboundFunctionWithParam(param1='a',param2='b')

We can also call POST for unbound the following is a unbound Action with params 

POST /odata/v4/catalog/unboundActionWithParam 
{
    "param1": "a",
    "param2": "b"
}

Bound and Unbound Function are part of the ODATA V4 and ODATA V2 Specifications.  
An example use case are custom create actions for the SAP Fiori elements UI. See Providing Services | capire for more information.

Creating our CAP Application.

1. Login to your CAP Application Studio. ￼

2. Open a terminal window.

3. Create a CAP application run the following.
    cds init books


We now have our starting point.
4. Go to Open Folder.

5. Search for the books project and open it.

As we can see our project and the foundation.

Creating our Database References.

We now need to start adding our files to enable the project.

6. Create a file under the folder /db and call it data-model.cds.
7. Cut and paste the following into the data-model.cds

 

namespace my.books;
  entity Books {
  key ID : Integer;
  title  : String;
  stock  : Integer;
}

 

8.   Create a directory in the /db folder and call it data.
9. Next Create a file in the data folder and call it my.bookshop-Books.csv.
10. Paste the following data into the file my.bookshop-Books.csv.

 

ID;title;stock
1;Wuthering Heights;100
2;Jane Eyre;500
3;Paul Newman;10
4;John Appy;4
1;Willow Tree;20
6;Small Talk;50
7;John Deer;10
8;Harry Jones;20
9;Snowing heights;40
10;Go Sailing;100
11;racing cars;50
12;Moving On;40
13;where are we;40
14;No place to go;60
15;SAP BAS Cap dev;50

 

Your project should look like this.
Creating Service Implementations.

11. Next go to the /srv folder and create a file and call it cat-service.cds.
12. We now need to create our Action and map it to a Service.
13. Copy and paste the following into cat-service.cds.

 

using my.books as my from '../db/data-model';
        
service CatalogService {
    type Result: {
        message: String;
        id: Integer;
        param1: String;
        param2: String;
    };

   
    /*Tested with readonly and not read only*/

     @readonly
    entity Books as projection on my.Books
        actions {
            action boundActionWithParam(param1:String, param2:String) returns Result;
            action boundActionWithoutParam() returns Result;
            function boundFunctionWithParam(param1:String, param2:String) returns Result;
            function boundFunctionWithoutParam() returns Result;
        };

        action unboundActionWithParam(param1:String, param2:String) returns Result;
        action unboundActionWithoutParam() returns Result;
        function unboundFunctionWithParam(param1:String, param2:String) returns Result;
        function unboundFunctionWithoutParam() returns Result;
}

Here we are defining the bound function as well as the unbound function.
Important to note as we can see from our cat-service.cds we have defined an action item and then we add function.
To further understand Actions & Functions please see Providing Services | capire

We can see how the Catalog Service in books is mapping the Parameter. 

14. Next in the srv folder create a file and call it cat-service.js.
15. Copy and paste the following into cat-service.js.

exports = async function () {

  //bound actions and functions
    this.on('boundActionWithParam', 'Books', req => {
        return {
            message: 'This is bound action with parameters!',
            id: req.params[0],
            param1:  req.data.param1,
            param2:  req.data.param2,

        }
    })
    this.on('boundActionWithoutParam', 'Books', req => {
        return {
            message: 'This is bound action without parameters!',
            id: req.params[0]
        }       
    })
    this.on('boundFunctionWithParam', 'Books', req => {   
        return {
            message: 'This is bound function with parameters!',
            id:  req.params[0],
            param1: req.data.param1,
            param2: req.data.param2
        }
    })
     this.on('boundFunctionWithoutParam', 'Books', req => {
        return {
            message: 'This is bound function without parameters!',
            id: req.params[0]
        }       
    })  

    //unbound actions and functions
    this.on('unboundActionWithParam', req => {
        return {
            message: 'This is unbound action with parameters!',
            param1: req.data.param1,
            param2: req.data.param2
        }
    })  
    this.on('unboundActionWithoutParam', req => {
        return {
            message: 'This is unbound action without parameters!',
        }
     })  
     this.on('unboundFunctionWithParam', req => {
        return {
            message: 'This is unbound function with parameters!',
            param1: req.data.param1,
            param2: req.data.param2
        }
    })
    this.on('unboundFunctionWithoutParam', req => {
        return {
            message: 'This is unbound function without parameters!'
        }
    })    
}

 

 Creating our HTTP ODATA V4 calls for Bound and Unbound.

16. Next create a new file in the root of the project and call it requests_v4.http
Note: The file name must have the extension of http to be an action item.

17. When creating an executable ODATA URL we add the ### this will automatically add
       Send Request there is no need to manually add Send Request manually.
18. Copy and paste the following into requests_v4.http.

###Bound Action with params
POST http://localhost:4004/odata/v4/catalog/Books(1)/CatalogService.boundActionWithParam

Content-Type: application/json
{ "param1": "a", "param2": "b" }

###Bound Action without params
POST http://localhost:4004/odata/v4/catalog/Books(1)/CatalogService.boundActionWithoutParam
Content-Type: application/json

###Bound Function with params
GET http://localhost:4004/odata/v4/catalog/Books(1)/CatalogService.boundFunctionWithParam(param1='a',param2='b')
Content-Type: application/json

###Bound Function without params
GET http://localhost:4004/odata/v4/catalog/Books(1)/CatalogService.boundFunctionWithoutParam()
Content-Type: application/json

###unbound Action with params
POST http://localhost:4004/odata/v4/catalog/unboundActionWithParam
Content-Type: application/json

{
    "param1": "a",
    "param2": "b"
}

###unound Action without params
POST http://localhost:4004/odata/v4/catalog/unboundActionWithoutParam
Content-Type: application/json

###Unbound Function withparams
GET http://localhost:4004/odata/v4/catalog/unboundFunctionWithParam(param1='a',param2='b')
Content-Type: application/json

###Unbound Function without params
GET http://localhost:4004/odata/v4/catalog/unboundFunctionWithoutParam()
Content-Type: application/json

Note: The URL Path in some cases the path may be different than the one shown
            if needed adjust the path that matches your environment.

We have completed the files that are needed, we now can start our application and start testing.
19 Open, a new terminal window.
20. Run the following command cds watch.

Note: If there is already a Service up and running the Port number maybe different
          test URL ports would have to be changed to reflect a different port number.

Executing our test ODATA V4 URL’s.

21. To check click on the Open in a New Tab. this action will take us to an automatically generated         start page.

Running our ODATA V4 Bound and Unbound tests.

22. Go back to our project.
23. Click on the send requests for Bound Action with params .

Next, we can test the Unbound ODATA V4.

24. Click on the Send Request for unbound with params.

Here is the results sent of the ODATA call

I used the local memory and SQLite to be test. 

Moving forward this is just a starting point with CAP using bound and unbound ODATA V4 API. There is some information in the SAP Blogs How to implement Bound functions and Unbound funct... - SAP Community as well in SAP CAP capire document Providing Services | capire

entry.title: Re: SAP Cloud Connector - websocket connection in SAP clo...
entry.content:
Hi Ankit, You might not be able to achieve that response time as all you are doing is replacing the Production Connector (which is acting as a MQTT to HTTP convertor) with a custom app that will work as a MQTT to HTTP convertor and will probably take a similar time if not longer (most of the time is taken negotiating the HTTP connection). Can you explain the full use case of what you are sending and why you are expecting a response so fast ? Using MQTT for sync communications is not a typical use case.

Also can you explain where you are going to deploy the MQTT client (BTP? POD Plugin?) . If its a POD plugin you don't need the cloud connector at all 😉 since the plugin is being executed on the local PC it has direct access to the local MQTT server. I have done this on several occasions. 

 

Thanks Kevin

entry.title: Re: SMB Adapter Feature: On-Premise Connectivity via SAP Cloud Connector
entry.content:
@KumarAakash To connect to an SMB share within your local network, you have 2 options:
1. You could, of course, use the IEC (edge integration cell). In which case, you need to ensure that your EIC can reach your local file system (network-wise)

2. Use the SMB adapter via the Cloud Connector. In which case, you will need to add a tcp entry in your Cloud Connector  

Both are valid approaches. The right approach for you will depend on a number of factors, like the availability of EIC , your landscape network, etc.

entry.title: Performance form, multiple managers
entry.content:
How can you capture comments from 2 managers on a performance form without using delegation?

scenario:

I report to manager A for half the year. I now report to manager B. Since the performance launch started after I was moved to the new manager my form is no longer visible to my old manager and cannot provide any comments. Does anyone have any ideas or use something other than delegation or matrix manager? Neither are viable options for our company.

We want both managers to be able to provide comments on the one form.

Thank you!'

Beth

entry.title: Re: Hierarchical Value Help in SAP Fiori Elements – Beyond Flat Value Help
entry.content:
Great, very useful blog @LiyaAjayan..!

But while trying the same ending with below two issues:

1. While using annotation  initialExpansionLevel: (suppose level 2), the value help doesn't load any data. It only loads in filter value help when this annotation is commented.

2. The Hierarchy shows level 1 only, when trying to click on drill down (expand option) hierarchies doesn't extend. Neither 'Expand entire tree' option works, the value help doesn't scroll even.

Any key insights ?

entry.title: In cycle counting, how do we add found material?
entry.content:
We are using SAP S4Hana Public Cloud and doing ad hoc cycle counts. We have discovered that the last physical inventory was done extremely poorly. So now we are doing cycle counting by trailer load. Each trailer is one storage bin and holds about 30 handling units. What do we do when we find material that has never been added to a handling unit and has never been counted?  Is there a best practice to add this material to the inventory? Or do we just drive the finance team crazy and write it in?

Thanks, Vicki

entry.title: Re: SuccessFactors Onboarding: Improving Form I-9 Document Upload Controls: What Changed & What’
entry.content: Thanks for sharing @ThomasBilbaugh

Jacson
SAP Support

entry.title: Re: SuccessFactors Onboarding: Improving Form I-9 Document Upload Controls: What Changed & What’
entry.content:
Hi, @ThomasBilbaugh ! Thanks for sharing, would you please mind confirming if this blog will be updated with the exact target date or Preview/Production patch dates? Thanks!

  • "This update we are also targeting for an early 2026 patch, and to globally revert our changes made in the early January update."

entry.title: Re: Restricting the BW queries exposed to Microsoft Power BI from B4HANA system
entry.content:
Very good article that helped me get started.  Thank you for sharing this.

Just a few points of clarification:

This enhancement does not allow us to filter the query list provided to Power BI, it only allows filtering of the infoproviders.

Using the code provided requires the infoproviders being listed fully in the roles with no wild cards.  The logic could be improved by taking the roles with wildcards and finding the infoproviders that match them and filling the catalogs table with those.

 

IF YOU WANT TO FILTER AT THE QUERY LEVEL, ENHANCE FUNCTION MODULE BAPI_MDPROVIDER_GET_CUBES

entry.content: Did you ever find a solution for this?

entry.title: Re: Does cal.sap.com run in SAP's corporate AWS acount?
entry.content: hi @MLES  cal.sap.com is hosted in Europe (rot) aka SAP data centers  . cheers!A

entry.title: Re: Early lot creation by product
entry.content:
I think the post he is referring, could be this one.
Which basically it says that Inspection lot type 04 (Goods Receipt for Production Order) is only created for the main product of a production order — not for co-products or by-products. Despite the configuration for the material .

  • One production order = one main GR-relevant material

entry.title: SAP Discrete manufacturing: Manufacturing date is automatically determined
entry.content:
Hello everyone.

Please could you help me?

I am facing this issue in my client. Every time I create a production order, when I release it the batch is created automatically, and also the field manufacturing date is populated with the system date.

My client requiere that the manufacturing date must be completed manually by users when they do the confirmation with the GR for the production order. 

I've tried to change the configuration in OPKP choosing C value in Date of Manufacture but it didn't work

I hope you can help me! Thanks

Best regards

entry.title: Re: What’s New in SAP Cloud Identity Access Governance
entry.content:
The link in the last sentence is not working. We don't have access to drafts

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_CLOUD_IDENTITY_ACCESS_GOVERNANCE?state=DRAFT

 

PS This is something that also nearly always happens when you post in What's New or Improvements on IAG, the link part should really have the draft part removed and link to public URLs.

entry.title: Re: SAP Process Automation: Free Tier Availability
entry.content:
UPDATE:

Actually, my workaround did work. It took about 30 minutes to install but it did install. So, if anyone ever has the problem, just Press the Create button in the "Instances and Subscriptions" page of the Subaccount, then select SAP Build Process Automation Plan: Free.

 

______________________

Hi Peter,

 

It seems the booster is no longer available in the 90 day BTP trails anymore. Is there a workaround? I've tried adding the manual Free plan Application directly in the BTP Trial account but it would just stay processing for ever

Booster no longer available...

Adding manually the application does not seem to work.

Thanks in advance

entry.title: BPA Unable to verify the signature of the archive (Transport Joule Skills)
entry.content:
Hi guys, could anyone help me out?

In SAP Build Process Automation (BPA), I’m trying to migrate/transport a Joule Skill project from Subaccount A (US, Azure) to Target Subaccount B (another region, US VA, AWS).

The import fails with this error: 

Unable to verify the signature of the archive. Make sure the landscape keys from landscape us21-prod are imported in your landscape, if not, contact DevOps. Ensure that the other potential reasons for this error are checked. This error could happen for the following reasons: Couldn't get key xxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxx version from credential store.

The same error - Transport and Upload proyect.

 

I’ve searched everywhere and couldn’t find anything useful.

I did find an SAP Note that suggests using a tenant in the same region, which is honestly very limiting because it would force us to change our entire landscape design just because of BPA:

 

 
https://me.sap.com/notes/3472593/E

Has anyone found a way to solve this (e.g., importing landscape keys / fixing credential store key access) and successfully transport BPA/Joule projects across regions?

I already opened a ticket with SAP, but I haven’t received an answer yet.

Thanks a lot!

How do I know if my SAP has this Add-on already? I just got access in a test environment where I work to understand how Data Masking works and I'm stuck after I enable the Global flag. ( BTW in SPRO mine was under ABAP Workbench not SAP NetWeaver).

I also don't know how to get the Technical information screen of a field of my choice

entry.title: Exporting ALV Reports to Excel in S/4HANA
entry.content:
I am displaying an ALV report in an S/4HANA environment, and when I go to the menu to export it to a spreadsheet (see image), only some fields are imported while others that are visible on the screen are not imported into the Excel file. Does anyone know how I can make all the fields be imported?
Note: Sorry that the menu in the screenshot is in Portuguese — even when logging in English, it did not translate. It was working fine on SAP ECC 6.0:

entry.title: Bloquear actualización del Address ID (CRD1) con Transaction Notification en SAP Business One HANA
entry.content:
Estoy trabajando con SAP Business One sobre HANA y necesito evitar que se actualice el campo Address(ID de dirección) en la tablaCRD1 , pero permitiendo

La creación de nuevas direcciones.

La actualizacion de otros campos del Socio de Negocio

El control lo estoy implementando mediante SBO_SP_TransactionNotification, validando el objeto Business Partner (OCRD) .

El problema es que, al intentar validar la modificación del ID de dirección, las condiciones terminan bloqueando otros cambios del socio de negocio o la creación.

¿Cuál es la mejor forma de detectar únicamente la actualización del ID de dirección en CRD1 dentro de Transaction Notification en HANA, sin afectar las demás operaciones?

Agradecería un ejemplo o recomendación de lógica para este escenario.

entry.title: Re: SAP Certification update: Practical exams roll out in 2026
entry.content:
Hi @Taofiksanni  and @Vifer1602 , thanks a lot for the positive feedback and for raising this issues. This is definitely no the expected behavior but a bug we've faced for a few learners and are looking into. Please accept our apologies as we navigate through the early phases of the new practical exams. It will be best to retake the exams. 
Thank you for your patience and for learning with SAP.

entry.title: Bloquear actualización del Address ID (CRD1) con Transaction Notification en SAP Business One HANA
entry.content:
Estoy trabajando con SAP Business One sobre HANA y necesito evitar que se actualice el campo Address (ID de dirección) en la tabla CRD1, pero permitiendo:

  • La creación de nuevas direcciones

  • La actualización de otros campos del Socio de Negocio

El control lo estoy implementando mediante SBO_SP_TransactionNotification, validando el objeto Business Partner (OCRD).

El problema es que, al intentar validar la modificación del ID de dirección, las condiciones terminan bloqueando otros cambios del socio de negocio o la creación.

¿Cuál es la mejor forma de detectar únicamente la actualización del ID de dirección en CRD1 dentro de Transaction Notification en HANA, sin afectar las demás operaciones?

Agradecería un ejemplo o recomendación de lógica para este escenario.

entry.title: Re: SAP Certification update: Practical exams roll out in 2026

entry.title: Re: SAP Cloud ALM - no supported use cases available for...

entry.title: Join us on March 5th: The Future of Intelligent Hiring with SmartRecruiters for SAP SuccessFactors
entry.content:
Talent acquisition is evolving faster than every – and to stay competitive, organizations need smarter, more connected, and more human hiring strategies. Join us for an engaging webinar where we’ll explore how intelligent hiring, AI-first innovations, and skills-based insights are reshaping talent acquisition and helping businesses make better hiring decisions at scale.

Join us on Thursday, March 5th at 11:00am EST to discover how SmartRecruiters for SAP SuccessFactors enables organizations to elevate hiring experiences, accelerate recruiting outcomes, and align every hire to business and workforce goals. See how SmartRecruiters works within the SAP SuccessFactors HCM suite to fuel hiring speed, talent quality, and long-term business success.

In this session, we’ll explore the latest trends shaping the future of hiring and showcase AI-first innovations designed to help organizations attract, engage, and hire the skills they need to stay ahead.

Key topics in this webinar include:

  • Emerging trends in talent acquisition and how AI is transforming hiring
  • SAP’s strategic AI and product innovations driving smarter hiring and business decisions
  • How intelligent hiring connects insights, skills, and automation to improve hiring outcomes
  • Real-world examples of how organizations are modernizing hiring across industries, regions, and hiring volumes
  • A live demo of AI-first and agentic capabilities in SmartRecruiters for SAP SuccessFactors to attract, hire, and retain top talent

Reserve your spot today! 

Don’t miss this opportunity to experience the future of intelligent hiring – and learn how SmartRecruiters for SAP SuccessFactors can help you hire faster, engage better candidates, and build a workforce that’s ready for what’s next.

entry.title: Re: Devtoberfest 2025 - Winners Announcement
entry.content: Congratulations Joffy

entry.title: Re: How can I allow a communication user to schedule a ba...
entry.content:
Hi Sandra,

Thanks for the detailed reply. I guess it's the wrong question to be asking.

The requirement is to allow a specific set of asynchronous work to occur as a reaction to a user's interaction. The user has the authorization to raise the event that triggers the asynchronous work to occur, but must not have the authorizations that are needed to perform asynchronous work itself.

We already have a roundtrip framework using an ABAP RAP based OData API v4 service that gets the processing of the asynchronous work to a Communication User with the appropriate authorizations to perform the tasks. The challenge is that the context is now in an ABAP RAP action execution. The asynchronous workload can be quite high, and it requires multiple commits during processing. This is not possible in the context of a RAP action execution. The bgPF framework could certainly be used to split up the work and I understand this work is done in a particular series, but all work packages need to report back to the overall execution to so it can calculate and set an overall result from all of the processing. An application job would work perfectly for this and it really irks me that this limitation exists. But I guess we'll need to keep searching for alternatives. Maybe hacking together some sort of DB persistence with a final bgPF 'finalize' processing step or something might work.

Thanks again.

entry.title: SAP Signavio Dictionaries
entry.content:
Hello,
I’m looking for some guidance on how you handle situations where swimlanes and dictionary entries are normally based on business roles, but certain tasks can be performed by anyone within a specific group or team, regardless of their formal position.
For example: Fire Wardens. This responsibility sits on top of a person’s nominal role, and it isn’t tied to a single area of the business.
Do you create a separate dictionary category for Responsibilities (e.g., Fire Warden), distinct from business roles?
If so:

How do you define and structure those responsibility entries?
And how does someone easily identify or contact the people assigned to that group when needed?

Thanks in advance for your insights.

entry.title: Re: Use SAP SuccessFactors Compensation to Provide Pay Equity Adjustments
entry.content:
@FL19  this set up is deliberate from my part as we are working on average salaries off the starting salaries.  If you wanted to use the modified salaries using this configuration, you would have look at doing a two step process where first all merit increases are applied and then the average male salary is calculated off of the modified salaries and loaded into the lookup table as appropriate.  I do think though for accurate reporting, you have to pick data as at a point in time either before or after all merit increases are applied. 

Keep in mind this example is simple and the blog was created to provide an example of what is possible using the Compensation module functionality.  It is very likely that real life examples will require more variables to determine the gender gap and both organisations and geographical regions will defer in their approaches to determine this.  Hope that helps.

entry.title: Fiori My Inbox: WITH_URL Annotation Always Shows URL Instead of Custom Text
entry.content:
Dear Community,

 

I am doing an enhancement in My Inbox app, where I want to add a url to a specific item row.  Here I am using two calculated fields, one to determine the text and another to determine the URL to be used. Now I have done the following in my CDS:

@Semantics.url: {

mimeType: 'url'

}

@ObjectModel.readOnly: true

@ObjectModel.virtualElementCalculatedBy: 'ABAP:ZCL_REVIEWINVOICE'

@ObjectModel.virtualElement: true

cast (' ' as abap.char(1024) )

as WEBSITEURL,

@ui.lineItem: [ {

position: 14,

importance: #HIGH,

type: #WITH_URL,

label : 'Webside URL' ,

value: 'REVIEWINVOICE' , //what users sees

url: 'WEBSITEURL' //actual link

} ]

@Semantics.url: {

mimeType: 'url'

}

@ObjectModel.readOnly: true

@ObjectModel.virtualElementCalculatedBy: 'ABAP:ZCL_REVIEWINVOICE'

@ObjectModel.virtualElement: true

cast( ' ' as abap.char(1024) ) as REVIEWINVOICE,

 

I am trying to display the virtual calculated text message REVIEWINVOICE and show the other virtual calculated URL WEBSITEURL when clicking. Problem is the value and the text seem to overwrite each other somehow. The URL loading seems to work only when i put WEBSITEURL in the value section.

Additionally if I comment the @Semantics.url for REVIEWINVOICE, I cannot click on the link anymore.

Any Ideas ideas what am I doing wrong here. I wanted to mention that in IF_SADL_EXIT_CALC_ELEMENT_READ~CALCULATE, I manage determine both the text REVIEWINVOICE and the URL for WEBSITEURL

Thanks and looking forward for any ideas on this topic.

entry.title: Pricing Integration using BAdI
entry.content:
Hi Team,

A pricing engine company would like to integrate their system to SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition and they would like to have their system integration to adhere to clean core principles of SAP for certification.

Originally it was setup for ECC which later converted to S/4HANA On Prem by using PRCD_ELEMENTS table manipulation to update the pricing in Sales Order but realize that this is not clean core compliant.

Due to time limitation, they are not able to create a new integration from scratch using SAP Cloud API and planning to use BAdI SD_SLS_MODIFY_ITEM to update the pricing conditions in sales orders. 

So the question would be, is this possible? And if it is, will this be clean core compliant?

Thank you.

Thanks for the useful info @AndreasSeifried . Quick question, how are we supposed to view the mass processes done in the old apps after this feature activation? At a minimum for reference for our team on what was mass changed, but also for audit.

Best,

Ryan

entry.title: Re: What’s New in SAP Cloud Identity Access Governance
entry.content: Thanks, should be fixed now!

entry.title: Re: using material type - service
entry.content: Olá, Entendo que mesmo que o Serviço não seja algo recebido fisicamente, a SAP poderá amadurecer o tema e reprogramar o MATERIAL TIPO SERV para utilizar, por exemplo, no Programa de Remessa. O Programa de Remessa é uma funcionalidade muito dinâmica, e justamente por isso poderemos ter tabelas de preços de fretes, serviços recorrentes, entre outros, onde poderíamos determinar os preços previamente na transação ME31L, para posteriormente programar a Divisão de Remessas para envio ao fornecedor conforme programação. Tentei fazer alguns testes e consultas Notas SAP, e não identifiquei oportunidades de realizar o Programa de Remessa com Material tipo SERV.

entry.title: Reading parent totals in SAC Data Actions
entry.content:
Hi folks.

Does anybody know a way in SAC to retrieve the value at a parent node in a Data Action? (or Calc Measure for that matter).

My specific use case is: I want to retrieve the (aggregated) value of a particular measure at the parent of a given member and write that value to another measure, across a set of multiple specific members.
What I wish I could do would simply be something like:
DATA(Measure=TargetMeasure) = RESULTLOOKUP(Measure=Amount, MyDim=MyDim.MyParent) but that isn't supported.

In KBA 279311, SAP notes the limitation that RESULTLOOKUP only supports leaf members, and states "The feature to Support Parent/Formula member in Resultlookup in Data Action is in our backlog for future consideration".
Well it's been around 7 years since that was issued and it still isn't available. It's proving unbelievably difficult to simply grab a value from a hierarchy parent.

That same KBA suggests manually recomputing by summing the leaf members to reconstruct the parent total, but how can we do that in a dynamic way? We can only really use BASEMEMBER in a MEMBERSET statement, and those can't be used in a dynamic fasion, e.g. within conditional logic or loops. Otherwise I would happily just loop over all my relevant members and redeclare MEMBERSET = BASEMEMBER(MyParent) for each one, but that doesn't work either.

Considering how central hierarchies are to how SAC works, it is mind-boggling that you cannot simply look up the value on a parent node in either Calculated Measures or Data Actions. I understand that the Query Engine and the Calc Engine work differently but come on!

The only way I can come up with so far is to maintain explicit rollup attributes for every individual level of the hierarchy against every leaf member and construct the values from the bottom up for all ancestor nodes, looping over all leaves but this seems frankly ridiculous to have to maintain for multiple hierarchies with multiple levels. 

If anybody knows a better way to approach this, I would be grateful to hear it.

Thanks in advance

entry.title: ECC license has expired
entry.content:
The SAP AE, has been working with to finalize the contracts to extend the ECC license.  This is delaying us from proceeding with a dry run of the ECC to S/4 Conversion.  This dry run is needed to validate the steps to ensure the actual Quality system conversion.  Is it possible to grant a 30-day temporary extension while the SAP AE works through this issue? 


--- Issue Date (UTC) ---
DIGITS-01-28 18:54:07 - DIGITS-02-03 18:54:07


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: ECC license has expired
entry.content:
As I tested from my side, it seems your contract has been updated and you should be able to generate permanent license key for SAP ERP 6.0 currently.

Attach the screenshot for your reference.

Could you please kindly have a try again?

Detail steps please refer to KBA 2366280 - How to request a license key for SAP S/4HANA (all versions) - SAP for Me

entry.content:

We are planning to build the BRIM (currently S/4HANA is in RISE) by enabling SAP Convergent Mediation and Convergent Charging using AWS EKS containerization installation.

 

But when we try to find containerization supportability for SAP Convergent Mediation and Convergent Charging, I am unable to get anything.

 

Few of the official docs/Notes from SAP and AWS available and confirming containerization for SAP Convergent Mediation but for Convergent Charging nothing I found.

Can you please check and help me to provide some official Notes/blogs, that will help us.

 

Thanks & Regards,

Ranjit Lingala

entry.title: SNOTE tcode not accessible after implementing SAP Note 3093855
entry.content:
Hello SAP Experts,

I am currently preparing for an upgrade to SAP S/4HANA 2023  and working on the Simplification Item Check
System current information : SAP_BASIS 756 SP04


Issue Description

To implement SAP Note 2502552 (required for the Simplification Item Check), I first implemented its prerequisite SAP Note 3093855 via transaction SNOTE.

However, after implementing SAP Note 3093855, transaction SNOTE is no longer accessible with Syntax error in program "SAPLSCWE" -  Type "CWB_T_NOTE_DWLD_HTML_RESP" is unknown.

Questions

  1. Are there any additional prerequisite notes or SP level requirements for this note?
  2. What is the recommended way to recover or fix SNOTE in this situation?
  3. Is there an alternative approach to proceed with the Simplification Item Check if SNOTE is not accessible?

    Thank you in advance for your support.

    Best regards,

entry.title: BRF+ rule table download - upload - GRC12 - SP25
entry.content:
Currently in our GRC12 system in we are using "ZGRAC_INITIATOR" as BRF+ rule table to take decision on which access request GRC should raise based on certain criteria i.e. new account, change account, transfer or lock account.

Now for some reason we have to update this ZGRAC_INITIATOR BRF+ table but while updating this table in DEV GRC system I found that one my colleague who was working in this project he did update the same table in 2020 and created one transport request which he did not moved further to production. So whatever changes he made those changes are still available in DEV only.

Now when I am doing my changes in DEV GRC system in the same table it is oblivious that it will also take the other changes which was done previously made in 2020.

I do not want to take those old changes as I am not aware what he did at that time.

can you please provide some guidance how we can move further in this case.

Is there any possibility that we can download current rule table from production or quality system and upload it in Dev system and then I will make my changes so that it will not impact the system.

If you have any other option, then please suggest.

Thanks 
Ravi Desai

#GRC12 #BRF+ #GRC #AccessControl

entry.title: Re: Opentext VIM - Validation Client
entry.content: Hi Gurus, i want to bypass Validation step please suggest necessary config

entry.title: Re: VIM Architecture
entry.content:
HI ,

Thanks for explaining the process, i want to bypass validation client skip 72 to 76 step and want to use Single click entry tool please suggest

entry.title: Re: Reading parent totals in SAC Data Actions
entry.content:
Hi @ebajema1 ,

There is a Global Configuration function available which can help you to achieve the desired result.

CONFIG.READ_CALCULATED_MEMBER_VALUES = ON

Below example copies the value from Parent Node to a Base Member.

Before DA Execution:

After DA Execution

Data Action Script

CONFIG.READ_CALCULATED_MEMBER_VALUES = ON
CONFIG.HIERARCHY.INCLUDE_MEMBERS_NOT_IN_HIERARCHY = [d/M1_PRODUCT]
MEMBERSET [d/Date] = BASEMEMBER([d/Date].[h/YQM] ,"[2025]" )
DATA([d/Measures]="QUANTITY",[d/M1_PRODUCT]="#") = RESULTLOOKUP([d/M1_PRODUCT]="P_TOT",[d/M1_GL_ACCOUNT]="Units Sold",[d/Measures]="QUANTITY")

Please refer to the below link for details.

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_ANALYTICS_CLOUD/00f68c2e08b941f081002fd3691d86a7/afe93e3cf1414a7b8419baad11cc066e.html?locale=en-US#config.read_calculated_member_values

Note from the link states the below current limitation 

  • This configuration function is only supported on SAP Analytics Cloud tenants based on SAP HANA Cloud.
  • Alternatively, you can achieve similar functional results by utilizing a data copy step within the data action to store calculation results before performing any additional dependent calculations or operations.

entry.title: We are running fund management quarterly. The next plan is to run fund management yearly in the new
entry.content: We are running fund management quarterly. The next plan is to run fund management yearly in the new financial year. Please suggest how to configuration year wise in Fund Management.

entry.title: I can't find SSCUI 102258 in the Central Business Configuration (CBC)
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community,

I am currently configuring the "Discounting of Long-Term Assets and Liabilities" within Advanced Valuation, following the SAP Help Portal documentation.

However, I am unable to locate the configuration activity "Define Discounting Rules" (ID: 102258). I have searched for this ID in both the Central Business Configuration (CBC) activity list and the general SSCUI list, but no results are found.

I have already verified that the relevant Finance scope items are active in my project. Could you please advise if this specific SSCUI has been deprecated, replaced by another activity, or if there are additional scope items/authorizations required to make it visible?

Any insights or guidance would be greatly appreciated.

- help portal : Configuration Requirements Checklist for Discounting Long-Term Assets and Liabilities | SAP Help Portal

entry.title: SAP IBP - Profit Center Description Not Appearing in Planning Objects
entry.content:
Business Impact:
The Profit Center Description attribute is not appearing in planning objects or planning views, even though it is correctly maintained in Profit Center master data and is visible as a referenced attribute. This gap prevents planners from validating Profit Center‑level targets, aggregations, and reporting in the IBP Excel UI. As a result, several planning activities that depend on Profit Center–based dimensions cannot proceed.

Operational Consequence:
Planners cannot differentiate or validate data by Profit Center, which directly impacts:

  • Inventory & SLOB Target value planning
  • Profit Center–level aggregation and disaggregation logic
  • Planning template validation and KPI roll‑ups
  • Downstream reports and decision‑making dependent on Profit Center segmentation

Urgency:
The issue blocks functional testing for Profit Center‑related requirements and is delaying project milestones. Resolution is required urgently to avoid slippage in current UAT and deployment timelines.

Expected Outcome from SAP:
We need SAP to identify why referenced attributes from Profit Center MDT are not being populated into planning objects despite correct configuration and activation. We expect guidance on model consistency checks, attribute propagation, or any required corrective actions at the application layer.

Summary of the Issue Observed in SAP IBP

The screenshots show the Master Data Maintenance view for a particular Master Data Type where a Reference Attribute has been configured but is not appearing in the Planning Object view.

Key Observations

  1. Master Data Maintenance (MDM) UI shows the attribute correctly

    • The attribute is visible in the MDM screen as a standard master data attribute.
    • This confirms that the attribute exists in the master data type definition.

  2. But the same attribute does not appear in the Planning Object view

    • When navigating to the Planning Object for the same entity, the attribute is missing.
    • This indicates a mismatch between the master data definition and the Planning Object model.

  3. Screenshots highlight the same field across 3 GUI views

    • All three slides display three different UI sections where the attribute is expected.
    • Each screenshot highlights the area where the attribute should appear but doesn’t.

  4. Likely root cause (based on typical IBP behavior)
    Although not in the file itself, the situation strongly suggests one of these IBP configuration gaps:

    • The attribute is not marked as “key figure relevant” or not included in the Planning Level.
    • The attribute may be missing in the Master Data Type – Planning Level assignment.
    • A cold model activation or attribute propagation may not have been refreshed.
    • The attribute is a Reference Attribute, which requires explicit inclusion in the Planning Model structure before it becomes visible.

Short explanation:

“The Reference Attribute is visible in Master Data Maintenance but does not appear in the Planning Object overview. This suggests that the attribute exists in the master data definition but is not propagated to the Planning Model due to missing assignment in the Planning Level, attribute relevance settings, or incomplete model activation.”

entry.title: How can I migrate SAP archives (SARA) from external maxdb database to new external filesystem?
entry.content:
Hey Guys, we are trying to move our existing SAP archived objects (SARA) from the existing content repository, which is a MaxDB installed as part of an external SAP Content Server, to a local filesystem on the same SAP Content Server.

The current content repository (OAC0) is a "Data Archiving (Document Area)" repository.

The target content repository (OAC0) is a "Document Management System (Document Area)" repository.

As per SAP Note: "3439593 - SAP Content Server - migrate a complete repository", scenario D, we have tried the use of custom program "Z_DOC_COPY" as described on SAP note 2774469 with no luck.

Has anyone else had to do this migration of archived objects between content repositories previously.

Cheers Nathan

entry.title: Re: SAP Cloud ALM Enablement Sessions in 2025
entry.content: Team - Can you please let me know the download links of all presentations

entry.title: Re: Reading parent totals in SAC Data Actions
entry.content:
@ebajema1 

There is an option to enable reading of data on parent/calculated members using a configuration. This is only supported on SAC tenant on HANA Cloud.

CONFIG.READ_CALUCATED_MEMBER_VALUES = ON

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_ANALYTICS_CLOUD/00f68c2e08b941f081002fd3691d86a7/afe93e3cf1414a7b8419baad11cc066e.html#config.read_calculated_member_values

The only other option to aggregate in a dynamic way is to create an attribute for each hierarchy which you covered.

Hope this helps !

Nikhil

entry.title: Re: Service broker error: Service broker apimanagement-apiportal-trial failed with: Forbidden
entry.content: LOL the API Management, API portal works on Azure Singapore

entry.title: Re: Reading parent totals in SAC Data Actions
entry.content: Perfect, thank you @spurwar! I didn't know that option existed 🙂

entry.title: Re: Reading parent totals in SAC Data Actions
entry.content: That's awesome, thank you @N1kh1l. It's a relief to see there is indeed a native way to achieve this.

entry.title: Re: Supporting I-9 Documents - Delete/Reupload Documents
entry.content:
Hi @emily_otis,

You can make use Trigger I-9 flow from manage data, to re trigger the I-9 flow and correct the data. 

You can trigger the reverification process manually by navigating to Admin Center Manage Data. In the Create New dropdown, select Trigger I-9 Flow. Enter the required details and select Save.

 

Thank you,

Regards,

Tabassum

entry.title: Bonus Buy Site group new site is not flown
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

Currently , we are facing one issue in SAP IS-Retail,newly created sites amending in the existing Bonus buy site group which the Bonus buy is already activated earlier.To Push the data we are following the below steps  . Deactivating the Bonus buy first then activating Bonus Buy again.We are pushing the data through  WPMI  &  custom Zreport .

As per Standard,Bonus buy data is not re-distributed  to the newly added site in the site group.Is there any other way around we can do this ? When ever there is a new site added in the site group that Bonus buy data should flow to the new site.How to achieve this?Please reply me with your valuable suggestions.

Thanks & Regards, 

Murali Mohan

SAP IS Retail

entry.title: Re: Exporting ALV Reports to Excel in S/4HANA
entry.content:
Hi, 

What S/4HANA Version are you on? ALV has been rewritten with 2023 and had a lot of issues with FPS 00 and 01. Have a look to available notes on component BC-SRV-ALV. 

Additionally: ALV has an own Export which Supports xlsx if the functions is enable by the Developer of the application. So the list menu should not be needed any more. I cannot tell if that is possible in your case.

entry.title: Re: Eclipse: SAP HANA Tools not available for 2025-12
entry.content:
Hi,
I ran into this as well and decided to check for a previous version.
In the end I installed the HANA tools of the previous release in eclipse 2025-12.
https://tools.hana.ondemand.com/2025-09

entry.title: Re: Can we round off the merit amount using standard meri...
entry.content: On top of David's comment : https://community.sap.com/t5/human-capital-management-blog-posts-by-sap/dates-and-roundings-in-compensation/ba-p/13461599 see "Rounding in standard recommendation fields" and note that it will also apply to all format sharing the same format (so most likely curSalary as well)

entry.title: CPE-UM Environment Activation Remains ‘Is Finished = No’ – How to Identify and Resolve the Issue
entry.content:
In the PaPM Cloud Universal Model, users frequently face an issue where the CPE–UM environment activation remains in the ‘Is Finished’ state as ‘No’ indefinitely, and no error logs are recorded for the Activate event.

In such cases, the environment activation does not end in either ‘Error’ or ‘Yes’ status. Instead, it remains ‘No’ forever after activation is triggered. In some scenarios, the environment activation ends with an error, but the error message itself is not meaningful. It does not clearly indicate the root cause or specify which function is causing the issue, making it difficult to identify configuration bugs.

In PaPM On-Premise, the system clearly highlights the function with errors using an error icon on the individual function. However, in the Universal Model, no such indication is available. Only the Runtime Status of a function is displayed as Current, Not Current, or Inactive, which is not sufficient to identify configuration issues or bugs.

Due to this limitation, users often end up raising an OSS ticket to resolve the issue.

However, this issue can often be resolved internally by using the Validate option available in the Manage Environment tile. The Validate option checks the entire environment configuration for inconsistencies and clearly reports (if any errors):

  1. Detailed error messages
  2. The affected Function ID(s)
  3. Relevant information in the event logs


For environments with a large number of functions and complex calculations, it is strongly recommended to validate the environment before activation. This helps identify configuration issues early and saves significant time and effort that would otherwise be spent troubleshooting activation failures.

Using the Validate option before activating the environment helps ensure configuration consistency and avoids unnecessary confusion during activation.

Conclusion
When a CPE–UM environment activation remains in Is Finished = No, it becomes difficult to identify the root cause due to limited error details. Using the Validate option in the Manage Environment tile helps detect configuration issues and provides clear error information before activation. Validating the environment in advance can save time and avoid unnecessary troubleshooting.

Thank you for reading this blog post.
If there are any questions or doubts, please feel free to leave a comment. I’ll be happy to help. Have a great day!
